sectionsUnavailable: Authenticated Sequencing.com sample report rows are not available yet.

No calibrated skin-health trait model, dermatology model, skincare-response model, UV-sensitivity model, skin-aging model, acne model, or melanoma or skin-cancer risk model is supplied with this fixture.
Skin health, skin type, UV sensitivity, skincare response, acne, skin aging, dermatology outcomes, and skin-cancer risk are not reported.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived HERC2/OCA2 rs12913832 AG call is available for pigmentation and eye-color context. This does not determine exact eye color, skin tone, skin type, skin health, UV sensitivity, or dermatology outcome.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: OCA2 and HERC2 are important in pigmentation biology, but skin and eye color are influenced by multiple genes and non-genetic factors.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived MC1R rs1805007 CT call is available for pigmentation, hair-color, and freckle context. This is not dermatology guidance, skincare guidance, UV-sensitivity guidance, or a melanoma or skin-cancer risk result.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: MC1R is a well-studied pigmentation gene, but many genes and non-genetic factors contribute to hair color, freckles, and related visible traits.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived EDAR hair-texture/thickness context row is available. This is educational context only and does not predict exact hair texture, skin health, or cosmetic outcome.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Hair texture and thickness have genetic contributions, but many genes and non-genetic factors can influence the trait.

unavailableTraits: Authenticated Sequencing.com sample report rows are not available yet.
Skin health, skin type, UV sensitivity, skincare response, acne, skin aging, dermatology outcomes, and melanoma or other skin-cancer risk are unavailable and not inferred.
Skin, pigmentation, freckles, and hair-context rows are limited to educational visible-trait context, not dermatology, skincare, medical-risk, ancestry, identity, or exact appearance interpretation.
plainEnglishLimits: Visible traits are influenced by multiple genes and non-genetic factors.
Direct-to-consumer trait tests provide estimates or context, not certainty about exact appearance.
This report must not be used to identify a person, infer race or ancestry, predict a child's appearance, guide skincare, diagnose skin conditions, or report disease risk.

references.title: MedlinePlus Genetics: Direct-to-consumer genetic test types

missingInputs: Authenticated sample-report sections and rows have not been extracted.
No calibrated skin-health trait probability model, dermatology model, skincare-response model, UV-sensitivity model, skin-aging model, acne model, or melanoma or skin-cancer risk model is supplied.
Skin health, skin type, UV sensitivity, skincare response, acne, skin aging, dermatology outcomes, and melanoma or other skin-cancer risk require separate validated evidence and are not inferred.
Many known and unknown contributors to pigmentation, freckles, hair texture, and other visible traits are not represented in this fixture.
uncertainty: Visible traits can be influenced by multiple genes, development, age, environment, and other non-genetic factors.
The significance of individual visible-trait markers may vary across populations and should not be treated as deterministic appearance, skincare, dermatology, or medical-risk prediction.
limitations: This validation result uses derived evidence only; raw genome data is not included.

The report is educational and does not identify a person, infer race or ancestry, predict a child's appearance, give skincare guidance, diagnose skin conditions, or report melanoma or other skin-cancer risk.
Authenticated Sequencing.com sample report rows are still pending.
